manual_title: Emergency Cardiovascular Resuscitation Pharmacology: A Technical Reference Manual

medications.name: Epinephrine
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required for cardiac arrest; administer undiluted as a direct rapid IV/IO push.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Initial Bolus: 1 mg IV/IO over 1–2 seconds. Repeat Interval: Every 3–5 minutes during cardiac arrest. Flush: Follow with a 20 mL NS flush and elevate extremity.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: High-dose regimens not routinely recommended.[cite: 1]
Extravasation via peripheral line can cause severe localized tissue necrosis; switch to central line post-ROSC if continuous infusion is initiated.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Amiodarone
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: First Bolus: Dilute 300 mg (2 vials) into 20–30 mL of D5W or NS. Second Bolus: Dilute 150 mg (1 vial) into 20–30 mL of D5W or NS.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: First Bolus: 300 mg IV/IO rapid push. Second Bolus: 150 mg IV/IO rapid push. Repeat Interval: Once after 3–5 minutes if VF/pVT persists.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Adsorption: Adheres to PVC containers; use non-PVC or glass if preparing post-ROSC maintenance infusions.[cite: 1]
Excipient alert: Contains polysorbate 80, which can induce profound hypotension post-ROSC.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Lidocaine
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required for cardiac arrest; administer undiluted as a direct rapid IV/IO push.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Initial Bolus: 1.0–1.5 mg/kg IV/IO rapid push. Repeat Interval: 0.5–0.75 mg/kg every 5–10 minutes. Max Dose: 3 mg/kg total.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Monitor for signs of Lidocaine toxicity (neurotoxicity: slurred speech, altered mental status, muscle twitching, seizures) post-ROSC.[cite: 1]
Reduce maintenance dosing in severe hepatic dysfunction or heart failure.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Magnesium Sulfate
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 2 g (4 mL of stock solution) into 10–20 mL of D5W or NS for rapid push in cardiac arrest.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Bolus Dose: 2 g IV/IO administered over 1–2 minutes in pulseless Torsades de Pointes.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Rapid administration in patients with a pulse causes profound vasodilation and hypotension.[cite: 1]
Monitor for neuromuscular blockade, loss of deep tendon reflexes, and respiratory depression post-ROSC.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Calcium Chloride
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required for emergency resuscitation; administer undiluted as a slow IV/IO push over 2–5 minutes.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Bolus Dose: 500 mg to 1000 mg (5–10 mL) IV/IO over 2–5 minutes.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Incompatibility: Extensively precipitates if mixed with Sodium Bicarbonate; thoroughly flush lines between administrations.[cite: 1]
Tissue Sclerosant: Severe tissue necrosis and sloughing if extravasated; central venous line preferred.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Sodium Bicarbonate
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required for acute emergency resuscitation; administer undiluted as a slow IV/IO push over 3–5 minutes.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Bolus Dose: 1 mEq/kg IV/IO slow push. Repeat Interval: 0.5 mEq/kg every 10 minutes based on arterial blood gas (ABG) and $pH$ tracking.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Chemical Precipitation: Forms insoluble calcium carbonate precipitates when combined with Calcium Chloride or Calcium Gluconate.[cite: 1]
Causes intracellular acidosis if ventilation is inadequate (shifts $CO_2$ balance).[cite: 1]

medications.name: Adenosine
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required; draw up undiluted. Prepare standard two-syringe rapid flush technique or a single syringe mixed with saline.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: First Dose: 6 mg IV rapid bolus over 1–2 seconds via proximal port, immediately followed by 20 mL NS flush. Second Dose: 12 mg IV rapid bolus after 1–2 minutes if conversion fails.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Transient Asystole: Warn patients of imminent, brief, distressing chest pain, dyspnea, or flushing.[cite: 1]
Dosing Alterations: Reduce initial dose to 3 mg if administered via a central line, or if patient is taking dipridamole/carbamazepine.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Amiodarone Infusion
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Loading Dose: Dilute 150 mg (3 mL) into 100 mL of D5W. Maintenance Infusion: Dilute 900 mg (18 mL) into 500 mL of D5W.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Loading Dose: 150 mg IV administered over 10 minutes (15 mg/min). Maintenance: 1 mg/min for the first 6 hours (33.3 mL/hr), then decelerate to 0.5 mg/min for the next 18 hours (16.6 mL/hr).[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Hypotension/Bradycardia: Rapid loading infusions provoke severe arterial hypotension. Slow or pause infusion if BP drops.[cite: 1]
Solvent Restriction: Incompatible with Normal Saline for long-term infusions; must use D5W and non-PVC sets.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Procainamide
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 1000 mg (10 mL) into 100 mL or 250 mL of D5W or NS.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Loading Infusion: 20–50 mg/min IV continuous infusion. Max Load: 17 mg/kg. Maintenance: 1–4 mg/min continuous infusion.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Discontinuation Criteria: Stop loading infusion immediately if: (1) Arrythmia suppresses, (2) Hypotension ensues, (3) QRS widens by $>50\%$, or (4) Max dose reached.[cite: 1]
Avoid in patients with a prolonged baseline QTc interval.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Diltiazem
clinical_indications: Rapid Ventricular Rate Control in AF / Flutter[cite: 1]
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Direct Bolus: Administer undiluted. Continuous Infusion: Dilute 125 mg (25 mL) into 100 mL of NS or D5W.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: First Bolus: 0.25 mg/kg actual body weight IV slow push over 2 minutes (typical dose 15–20 mg). Second Bolus: 0.35 mg/kg IV after 15 minutes if rate control is inadequate. Continuous Infusion: Initial rate of 5–15 mg/hr.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Contraindicated in decompensated heart failure, severe LV dysfunction ($EF < 40\%$), and Wolff-Parkinson-White (WPW) syndrome with pre-excited AF.[cite: 1]
Anticipate peripheral vasodilation and negative inotropic hypotension.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Metoprolol Tartrate
clinical_indications: Rate Control in AF / Hyperadrenergic SVT[cite: 1]
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required; administer undiluted as a slow IV push.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Bolus Dose: 5 mg IV slow push over 2 minutes. Repeat Interval: Every 5 minutes as needed. Max Dose: 15 mg total (3 doses).[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Avoid in cardiogenic shock, active bronchospasm (severe asthma/COPD), advanced AV block, or decompensated heart failure.[cite: 1]
Monitor for abrupt bradycardia or drop in cardiac output.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Ibutilide
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: May administer undiluted, or dilute 1 mg (10 mL) into 50 mL of NS or D5W.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Dosing ($\ge 60\text{ kg}$): 1 mg IV infused continuously over 10 minutes. Dosing ($< 60\text{ kg}$): 0.01 mg/kg IV over 10 minutes. Repeat Interval: Repeat once after 10 minutes if arrhythmia persists.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Proarrhythmia Risk: High risk of inducing Torsades de Pointes. Continuous ECG monitoring is mandatory during infusion and for at least 4 hours post-infusion.[cite: 1]
Correct $K^+$ and $Mg^{2+}$ levels prior to administration.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Atropine Sulfate
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required; administer undiluted as a rapid IV push.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Bolus Dose: 1 mg IV rapid push. Repeat Interval: Every 3–5 minutes as clinically indicated. Max Cumulative Dose: 3 mg total.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Paradoxical Bradycardia: Doses $< 0.5\text{ mg}$ or slow administration can cause central vagal stimulation, paradoxical heart rate slowing, or advanced AV block.[cite: 1]
Ineffective in type II second-degree or third-degree AV blocks with wide QRS complexes.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Dopamine Infusion
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Use premixed container; if compounding, add 400 mg (10 mL vial) into 250 mL of D5W or NS.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Continuous Infusion: Chronotropic / beta-adrenergic range of 2–10 mcg/kg/min. Titrate up in increments of 2–5 mcg/kg/min based on hemodynamics.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Arrhythmogenic Potential: Highly tachyarrhythmogenic; can provoke ventricular ectopy and atrial fibrillation.[cite: 1]
Extravasation risk causes localized skin necrosis; phentolamine is the antidote. Central line access preferred.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Epinephrine Infusion
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Withdraw 1 mg (1 mL) of stock and dilute into 250 mL of NS or D5W.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Continuous Infusion: 2–10 mcg/min non-weight-based continuous titration. Adjust by 1–2 mcg/min every 3–5 minutes to achieve targeted MAP/HR.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Extensively increases myocardial oxygen consumption ($MVO_2$); may induce localized subendocardial ischemia or tachyarrhythmias.[cite: 1]
Central line access strongly preferred; monitor peripheral site closely if used emergency-short term.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Isoproterenol
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 1 mg (5 vials) into 250 mL of D5W or NS.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Continuous Infusion: Initial rate of 2 mcg/min. Titrate within a range of 2–10 mcg/min based on heart rate response.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Profound Vasodilation: Pure beta-agonist effects ($\beta_1 + \beta_2$) can cause marked peripheral vasodilation and a drop in diastolic blood pressure.[cite: 1]
Contraindicated in ischemic heart disease due to the potential for severe tachycardia and ischemia.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Etomidate
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required; administer undiluted via rapid IV push.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Induction Bolus: 0.3 mg/kg IV push over 30–60 seconds. Calculated average adult dose range is 20–30 mg.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Hemodynamically stable, neutral induction profile.[cite: 1]
Inhibits $11-\beta$-hydroxylase, causing transient adrenal suppression; use with caution in severe septic shock states.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Ketamine
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required; can administer undiluted for RSI induction.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Induction Bolus: 1.5–2.0 mg/kg IV push over 60 seconds. Infusion (Sedation): 0.5–2.0 mg/kg/hr.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Sympathomimetic: Stimulates endogenous catecholamine release, raising blood pressure and heart rate. Ideal for shock states, but caution is warranted in severe ischemic coronary syndrome.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Propofol
medications.available_stock_dosage_forms: 1000 mg / 100 mL Vial (10 mg/mL) Lipid emulsion[cite: 1]
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required for direct infusion; administer undiluted via dedicated lines.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Induction Bolus: 1.5–2.5 mg/kg IV push. Continuous Infusion: 5–50 mcg/kg/min; titrate by 5 mcg/kg/min every 5 minutes.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Myocardial Depressant: Provokes peripheral venodilation and negative inotropic hypotension. Contraindicated in shock.[cite: 1]
PRIS: Monitor triglycerides and CK if infused $>48\text{ hours}$ at high doses (Propofol Infusion Syndrome).[cite: 1]

medications.name: Succinylcholine
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required; administer undiluted via rapid IV push.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Paralytic Bolus: 1.5 mg/kg IV rapid push. Complete neuromuscular blockade is achieved within 45–60 seconds.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Malignant Hyperthermia: Potential trigger.[cite: 1]
Hyperkalemia: Contraindicated in patients with severe burns, crush injuries, or subacute denervating neuromuscular disease due to the risk of hyperkalemic cardiac arrest.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Rocuronium Bromide
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required; administer undiluted via rapid IV push.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Paralytic Bolus: 1.0–1.2 mg/kg IV rapid push for emergency intubation sequencing. Prolonged duration of action (45–60 mins).[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Prolonged paralysis occurs in hepatic or renal impairment.[cite: 1]
Ensure continuous, deep sedation is initiated immediately following paralytic administration.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Naloxone
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 0.4 mg into 9 mL of NS to allow for careful micro-dosing titration if patient is not in cardiac arrest.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Apnea/Respiratory Depression: 0.04–0.4 mg IV/IO titrated slowly to restore adequate respiratory drive without triggering full withdrawal.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Acute Pulmonary Edema: Rapid, high-dose reversal can cause severe hyperadrenergic withdrawal, leading to acute non-cardiogenic pulmonary edema and severe agitation.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Flumazenil
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required; administer undiluted over 15–30 seconds.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Initial Bolus: 0.2 mg IV over 15 seconds. Repeat Interval: 0.3 mg after 30 seconds, then 0.5 mg every 1 minute. Max Dose: 3 mg total.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Seizure Risk: Can precipitate refractory, life-threatening seizures in patients with chronic benzodiazepine dependence or concurrent tricyclic antidepressant (TCA) overdose.[cite: 1]

sections.section_note: All concentrations calculated below represent standard preparation methods. In clinical practice, high-concentration central line mixtures ('double-strength') are frequently utilized to prevent fluid overload.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Norepinephrine
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 4 mg (4 mL) into 250 mL of D5W or NS. D5W prevents oxidation of the drug.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Continuous Infusion: Initial rate of 0.05–0.1 mcg/kg/min or 2–5 mcg/min non-weight-based. Titrate dynamically to maintain a targeted $MAP \ge 65\text{ mmHg}$.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Ischemic digital or mesenteric vasospasm can occur at high doses.[cite: 1]
Vesicant: Central line access is mandatory. If peripheral extravasation happens, infiltrate local tissue with 5–10 mg phentolamine.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Dobutamine
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 250 mg (20 mL) into 250 mL of D5W or NS.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Continuous Infusion: Infuse at 2–20 mcg/kg/min. Titrate by 2.5 mcg/kg/min every 10 minutes based on cardiac index and tissue perfusion.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Tachyarrhythmias: Can prompt significant sinus tachycardia, ventricular ectopy, or rapid atrial fibrillation.[cite: 1]
Vasodilation: Mild $\beta_2$-mediated peripheral vasodilation may worsen hypotension if left ventricular filling pressures are inadequate.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Milrinone
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 20 mg (20 mL) into 100 mL of D5W or NS.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Continuous Infusion: 0.25–0.75 mcg/kg/min maintenance rate. Maintenance titration adjustments are made slowly over hours due to its long half-life.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Hypotension: Accumulates in renal impairment. Severe systemic vasodilation can cause hypotension; avoid loading boluses in acute cardiogenic shock.[cite: 1]
Requires parallel vasopressor support if MAP drops.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Vasopressin
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 20 units (1 mL) into 100 mL of NS or D5W.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Continuous Infusion: Fixed, non-titrated adjunct rate of 0.03–0.04 units/min for septic shock vasoactive optimization.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Do not titrate up rapidly like catecholamines; high doses ($>0.04\text{ units/min}$) provoke severe myocardial ischemia, splanchnic hypoperfusion, and digital necrosis.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Phenylephrine
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 10 mg (1 mL) into 250 mL of NS or D5W.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Continuous Infusion: Initial rate of 0.5–2.0 mcg/kg/min or 40–100 mcg/min non-weight-based. Titrate to target MAP.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Reflex Bradycardia: Pure alpha-1 agonist effects lead to a rise in systemic vascular resistance, which can trigger a vagally-mediated drop in heart rate.[cite: 1]
Reduces stroke volume in patients with pre-existing left ventricular dysfunction.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Nitroglycerin
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 50 mg (10 mL) into 250 mL of D5W or NS.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Continuous Infusion: Initial rate of 5–10 mcg/min. Titrate up by 5–10 mcg/min every 3–5 minutes based on chest pain level and systemic blood pressure parameters.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: PDE-5 Inhibitor Contradiction: Do not administer if sildenafil, vardenafil, or tadalafil was used within 24–48 hours due to the risk of profound, refractory hypotension.[cite: 1]
Adsorption: Must be prepared in non-PVC glass or polyolefin infusion containers.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Labetalol HCl
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Bolus: Administer undiluted. Continuous Infusion: Dilute 200 mg (40 mL) into 160 mL of D5W or NS.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Intermittent Bolus: 20 mg IV slow push over 2 minutes. Repeat with escalating doses (40 mg, then 80 mg) every 10 minutes as needed (Max 300 mg). Continuous Infusion: 1–8 mg/min titrated to target blood pressure.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Dual alpha/beta blocker ($1:7\text{ IV}$ ratio).[cite: 1]
Avoid in decompensated heart failure, severe reactive airway disease, advanced heart block, or bradycardia.[cite: 1]
Monitor for unexpected precipitous drops in cardiac output.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Nicardipine HCl
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 25 mg (10 mL) into 240 mL of NS or D5W.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Continuous Infusion: Initial rate of 5 mg/hr. Titrate up by 2.5 mg/hr every 5–15 minutes to a maximum rate of 15 mg/hr until target BP is achieved.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Phlebitis: Highly irritating to peripheral veins; rotate peripheral access sites every 12 hours or switch to central venous delivery if long-term support is required.[cite: 1]
Avoid in advanced aortic stenosis.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Sodium Nitroprusside
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: Dilute 50 mg (2 mL) into 250 mL of D5W only.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Continuous Infusion: Initial rate of 0.3 mcg/kg/min. Titrate by 0.5 mcg/kg/min every 5 minutes based on real-time intra-arterial line tracking (Max 10 mcg/kg/min).[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Cyanide/Thiocyanate Toxicity: Metabolized to cyanide. Toxicity presents as unexplained metabolic acidosis, altered mental status, and lactate elevation; monitor closely if used $>48\text{ hours}$ or in renal impairment.[cite: 1]
Light Sensitivity: Wrap the infusion bag in light-protective foil.[cite: 1]

medications.name: Esmolol HCl
medications.preparation_methods_and_preferred_solven: None required for the 10 mg/mL commercial vial formulation; can run directly as an infusion.[cite: 1]
medications.calculated_dosing_and_administration_gui: Loading Bolus: Optional 500 mcg/kg IV push over 1 minute. Continuous Infusion: 50–300 mcg/kg/min; titrate by 50 mcg/kg/min every 5 minutes based on target heart rate and blood pressure parameters.[cite: 1]
critical_safety_alerts: Ultra-Short Acting: Half-life is roughly 9 minutes. If severe hypotension or bradycardia occurs, turning off the infusion results in rapid clinical reversal within minutes.[cite: 1]
Highly accurate for testing beta-blocker tolerance.[cite: 1]

cross_references: For specific multi-center heart failure outcome registries and tracking protocols, see details referenced in the data framework under THESUS-HF II protocols.[cite: 1]

For automated clinical calculators managing weight-based dose optimization software systems, consult code frameworks labeled under the reference files "watermarked_img_15605539625956534618.png", "watermarked_img_13413688051120390643.png", and "watermarked_img_8095023707865201722.png". Ensure concentration variables map directly to the calculated outputs detailed in Sections 3, 5, and 6 above.[cite: 1]
